methodology: Bottom-up S-curve displacement model. Maps technology adoption curves to sector-by-sector fossil fuel displacement, converts cumulative emissions to temperature via TCRE (IPCC AR6). This is a simplified thought experiment, not a full integrated assessment model.

assumptions.non_co2_forcing: 0.5°C baseline, declining 40% by 2100 as methane reduces

scurve_parameters_used.aviation: Assumed logistic K=80, r=0.20, t0=2042 (SAF adoption analogy)
scurve_parameters_used.shipping: Assumed logistic K=70, r=0.18, t0=2044 (alt fuel adoption)
scurve_parameters_used.industry: Assumed logistic K=60, r=0.07, t0=2048 (green H2 + EAF)
scurve_parameters_used.buildings: Assumed logistic K=80, r=0.15, t0=2035 (heat pumps)

current_policies.description: UNEP 2024 Emissions Gap Report — assumes current implemented policies continue

sources: S-curve parameters fitted from OWID/IEA data
